sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.72 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: We say that a group is a if it is finitely generated, not cyclic, and all of its proper non-trivial subgroups are isomorphic to each other. [r]{a)} Do there exist amenable torsion-free Tarski monsters? [r]{b)} Do there exist amenable Tarski monsters of prime exponent? {{ D.\,Kielak}}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
